title: Create POST /api/vault/documents endpoint
implementation_notes: Body: { title: string, content?: string }. Validate: title must be non-empty, max 200 chars.

Use resolveSharedVaultPath() to get vault root. Documents go in the vault root itself (not vault/shared/) — i.e., vaultRoot directly, because the vault explorer reads arbitrary .md files from the Obsidian vault root.
Derive filename: slugify(title) + '.md'. If the file already exists, append -2, -3, etc.
File content: a simple markdown file with a # {title} heading followed by a blank line and content (empty string if omitted).
Return 201 with { id, title, sourcePath, createdAt }.
Add a unit test: successful creation, duplicate title gets suffix, empty title → 400.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/vault/documents with { title: 'My Note' } writes {vaultRoot}/my-note.md and returns 201.
Second POST with the same title writes my-note-2.md without error.
GET /api/vault/documents after creation includes the new file.
completion_summary: Added POST /api/vault/documents with title validation, collision suffixing, and tests covering creation, suffix behavior, and empty-title rejection.
